sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Imperial Petroleum (IMPP) price target increased by 33.33% to 8.16
feed.summary: The article states that Imperial Petroleum's (IMPP) price target has been increased by 33.33%, bringing it to $8.16. No further details or context are provided.

feed.title: J.P.Morgan, Morgan Stanley urge buying the dip as US earnings stay resilient
feed.summary: J.P.Morgan and Morgan Stanley recommend buying market dips, citing resilient U.S. corporate earnings growth despite geopolitical tensions stemming from the Middle East conflict. The S&P 500 has shown strength, outperforming European and emerging markets, with earnings growth estimates increasing for Q1 2026. Both firms highlight opportunities in cyclical sectors and quality growth stocks, even as their regional preferences for equities differ.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Enphase Energy, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH) alleging violations of federal securities laws. The lawsuit claims Enphase overstated its ability to manage channel inventory and mitigate effects from the termination of the 25D Credit, thereby overstating its financial and operational prospects. Investors who purchased Enphase securities between April 22, 2025, and October 28, 2025, are encouraged to join the case, with a lead plaintiff deadline of April 20, 2026.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Files Class Action Against Hercules Capital
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has filed a class action lawsuit against Hercules Capital, Inc., alleging that the company's deal sourcing involved copying Google Ventures' investments and that its valuation team lacked proper oversight. The lawsuit claims that these practices could lead to inaccurate valuations of Hercules Capital's software debt portfolio, potentially impacting investor confidence. Investors who purchased securities between May 1, 2025, and January 27, 2026, have until May 21, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: Baxter Showcases Recent Innovations at AORN Global Surgical Conference & Expo 2026 (1)
feed.summary: Baxter International Inc. is exhibiting its latest surgical innovations at the AORN Global Surgical Conference & Expo 2026. Highlights include the AAT XR, a next-generation spine surgical table designed to enhance efficiency and patient safety during spine procedures, and the Dynamo Series smart stretcher, aimed at improving patient care and reducing burden on care teams. Baxter also features its Advanced Surgery portfolio of hemostatic and sealing agents.

feed.title: PACCAR (NASDAQ:PCAR) Price Target Raised to $130.00 at Citigroup
feed.summary: Citigroup has increased its price target for PACCAR (NASDAQ:PCAR) to $130.00 from $125.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating. This new target suggests a 3.08% potential upside from the current share price. Despite some analysts having a "Hold" consensus and an average price target of $117.42, PACCAR reported Q1 EPS of $1.06 (in line) and revenue of $6.82 billion (exceeding expectations), though revenue was down 15.1% year-over-year. Insiders have also sold approximately $16.9 million worth of stock in the last 90 days.

feed.title: Alphabet Inc. Class A stock (US02079K3059): Is AI dominance strong enough to sustain premium valuation?
feed.summary: This article analyzes whether Alphabet Inc.'s AI dominance can sustain its premium valuation, focusing on its core advertising and cloud businesses, as well as emerging ventures in AI and autonomous driving. It discusses the company's competitive advantages, strategic priorities, and financial performance, alongside potential risks such as antitrust scrutiny and evolving privacy regulations. The analysis also incorporates favorable analyst views while advising investors to watch for key indicators like earnings reports, regulatory rulings, and product launches.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges PayPal Holdings, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against PayPal Holdings, Inc. alleging that the company made materially false and misleading statements regarding its projected revenue outlook, anticipated growth, and the achievability of its growth strategy. Investors who purchased PayPal securities between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026, are encouraged to join the case and may request to be appointed as lead plaintiff by April 20, 2026. The firm operates on a contingency fee basis, seeking to recover damages for investors who suffered losses.

feed.title: Lululemon Stock Falls After Texas Opens PFAS Investigation Into Apparel
feed.summary: Lululemon's stock declined after Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton announced an investigation into the company's athletic apparel for the presence of PFAS "forever chemicals," which are linked to various health risks. This probe is part of a broader series of investigations in Texas targeting consumer products and adds to existing pressures on Lululemon, including slowing sales and a CEO search. Other companies like WK Kellogg and Procter & Gamble have also been subject to similar Texas investigations.

feed.title: Johnson & Johnson reports positive Varipulse results at EHRA 2026
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson (J&J) announced positive 12-month interim results from its VARIPURE post-market study of the Varipulse pulsed field ablation (PFA) system for AFib. The study demonstrated high effectiveness (84.2% freedom from atrial arrhythmia recurrence) and a strong safety profile (0.8% primary adverse event rate) across 1,023 patients in Europe. These findings, presented at the EHRA 2026 meeting, highlight Varipulse's versatility and performance in a real-world clinical setting.

feed.title: Can Hasbro Stock Continue Its Last Year’s 80% Rally?
feed.summary: Hasbro has undergone a significant transformation, with its Wizards of the Coast and Digital Gaming segment now driving nearly half of total revenue and most of its operating profit, significantly boosting its financial performance. Despite a recent cybersecurity incident and potential drag from Consumer Products, analyst targets suggest meaningful upside, positioning the company as potentially undervalued compared to its gamified entertainment peers. The continued success of Magic: The Gathering, new game launches, and the Harry Potter license are crucial for sustaining its rally and achieving expected growth into 2027.

feed.title: Conagra Brands Names John Brase as President and CEO
feed.summary: Conagra Brands has appointed John Brase as its new president and CEO, effective June 1, 2026. Brase, who previously served as president and COO of The J.M. Smucker Co., brings over 35 years of consumer goods experience to the role. He will succeed Sean Connolly, who is stepping down after more than a decade of leadership.

feed.title: Hudson Technologies Appoints Alan Sheriff and Jeffrey Feeler as Independent Directors to Drive Strategic Growth Initiatives
feed.summary: Hudson Technologies (NASDAQ: HDSN) has appointed Alan Sheriff and Jeffrey Feeler as new independent directors to its Board, effective April 13, 2026, to help drive strategic growth initiatives. Sheriff brings extensive experience in investment banking and capital markets, while Feeler offers significant operational and financial management expertise, particularly in environmental services. Concurrently, long-standing board member Vincent Abbatecola has resigned after more than three decades of service to the company.

feed.title: Investment Strategies for Volatile Markets: Analyzing Coca-Cola, Chevron, McDonald's - News and Statistics
feed.summary: This article examines investment strategies for volatile markets, suggesting that short-term declines can be opportunities for long-term asset acquisition. It highlights Coca-Cola (KO) and Chevron (CVX) as strong long-term holdings in Berkshire Hathaway's portfolio, noting their consistent dividends and supportive market factors. McDonald's (MCD) is also presented as a strong investment candidate due to its brand, cash flow, and focus on shareholder returns, despite not being a current Berkshire Hathaway holding.

feed.title: GXO Schedules First Quarter 2026 Earnings Conference Call for Wednesday, May 6, 2026
feed.summary: GXO Logistics, Inc. (NYSE: GXO) announced its first quarter 2026 earnings conference call and webcast will be held on Wednesday, May 6, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time. The company's financial results will be released after market close on Tuesday, May 5, 2026. Interested parties can access the call via a toll-free number or a live webcast on the company's investor relations website.

feed.title: Why a $110M Louisiana wealth team left LPL for Ameriprise
feed.summary: Pinnacle Wealth Management, a financial advisory practice with over $110 million in assets, has moved from LPL Financial to Ameriprise Financial. The Lafayette, Louisiana-based team, led by Vincent Lauer, made the switch to leverage Ameriprise's advanced technology and service offerings to enhance the client experience and improve operational efficiency. This move reflects Ameriprise's continued success in attracting experienced financial advisors looking for robust digital capabilities and comprehensive support to grow their businesses.

feed.title: UBS Adjusts Norwegian Cruise Line PT to $22 From $27, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: UBS has adjusted its price target for Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NCLH) to $22, down from $27, while maintaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This revision reflects a change in the firm's outlook for the cruise operator.

feed.title: Adobe and Figma initiated with Neutral ratings from BTIG
feed.summary: BTIG has initiated coverage of Adobe (ADBE) and Figma (FIG) with Neutral ratings. Both digital content creation software firms saw their shares rise approximately 4% in Monday's noon trading following the announcement. Adobe has historically maintained a significant market share in its sector.

feed.title: Autodesk appoints Mike Kelly as Chief Information Officer
feed.summary: Autodesk has appointed Mike Kelly as its new Chief Information Officer, effective April 13, 2026. Kelly will be responsible for leading the company's enterprise technology strategy, AI adoption, and digital employee experience. His appointment is expected to strengthen Autodesk's technology leadership as it prepares for further growth and leverages AI.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific’s PPD Clinical Research Business Collaborates with HealthVerity to Expand Real-World Data Capabilities
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific's PPD clinical research business has announced a strategic collaboration with HealthVerity to significantly expand its real-world data (RWD) capabilities. This agreement provides enterprise-level access to HealthVerity’s TaXOnomy claims dataset, covering over 270 million de-identified U.S. patient lives, and a marketplace of additional curated data sources. The collaboration aims to enhance data-driven clinical development, optimize trial feasibility and recruitment, and strengthen real-world evidence generation for biopharma sponsors.

feed.title: Lowey Dannenberg Notifies ImmunityBio, Inc. (“ImmunityBio” or the “Company”) (NASDAQ: IBRX) Investors of Securities Class Action Lawsuit and Encourages Investors with more than $100,000 in Losses to Contact the Firm
feed.summary: Lowey Dannenberg P.C. has filed a class action lawsuit against ImmunityBio, Inc. (NASDAQ: IBRX) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between January 19, 2026, and March 24, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that ImmunityBio materially overstated the capabilities of its product, Anktiva, leading to significant investor losses. Investors who lost over $100,000 are encouraged to contact the firm, with a deadline of May 26, 2026, to serve as Lead Plaintiff.

feed.title: Chipotle to Open New Restaurant in Melbourne
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill is set to open a new restaurant in Melbourne, Florida, on April 14, 2026. Located at 4521 Andromeda Road, this new establishment will feature a drive-thru pickup lane and will be open daily from 10:45 a.m. to 10 p.m. This expansion is part of Chipotle's strategy to increase its presence in Brevard County, capitalizing on the growing demand for fast-casual dining and quality Mexican cuisine.

feed.title: CAT Holds Dividend Steady For Now: Is a Hike Around the Corner?
feed.summary: Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) has maintained its quarterly dividend at $1.51 per share, continuing an impressive streak of 32 consecutive years of dividend increases, solidifying its status as an S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrat. Despite a period of declining revenues, the company raised its dividend in the past, demonstrating confidence in its long-term financial health. With improving operational momentum, record backlogs, and favorable market trends, Caterpillar is well-positioned for another potential dividend hike in the near future.

feed.title: UBS Adjusts Equifax Price Target to $220 From $230, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: UBS has adjusted its price target for Equifax (NYSE: EFX) to $220, down from the previous $230. Despite this change, the firm has decided to maintain a Buy rating on the company's stock. This indicates a continued positive outlook for Equifax by UBS, even with the revised price target.

feed.title: Halliburton Wins Multibillion-Dollar YPF Fracking Contract in Argentina
feed.summary: Halliburton Co. has secured a multibillion-dollar, multi-year contract from Argentina's state-run oil company YPF. Under this agreement, Halliburton will exclusively provide bundled fracking services in the Vaca Muerta shale patch, marking the international debut of its Zeus electric-powered fracturing technology.

feed.title: Textron wins $150M contract for T-6 aircraft support services
feed.summary: Textron Aviation Defense LLC, a subsidiary of Textron Inc., has secured a five-year U.S. government contract worth over $150 million to provide engineering and program management services for the T-6A, T-6B, and T-6D aircraft fleets used by the U.S. Air Force, Navy, and Army. This contract extends existing services for an additional five years, increasing the total cumulative contract ceiling to $510 million. The company's stock currently trades below its fair value, indicating a potentially undervalued opportunity in the aerospace sector.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics (TPL) adds to Texas Pacific Land stake with open-market buy
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a significant owner of Texas Pacific Land Corp (TPL), made a small open-market purchase of 1 common share on April 10, 2026, at $407.06. This transaction increased its direct holdings to 3,435,494 shares. The filing also references an amended Schedule 13D from March 26, 2026, which reported its beneficial ownership of 10,050,070 shares.

feed.title: Will CMS Energy (CMS) Beat Estimates Again in Its Next Earnings Report?
feed.summary: CMS Energy (CMS) is projected to beat its upcoming earnings estimates, continuing a trend from its previous two reports. The company has a positive Earnings ESP of +10.35% and a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), indicating a high probability of exceeding expectations. Investors should consider these factors ahead of its next earnings release scheduled for April 28, 2026.

feed.title: Oppenheimer Upgrades Blackstone to Outperform, Calling It the Premier Franchise at a Bargain Price
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has upgraded Blackstone (BX) stock to Outperform, citing its status as a "premier franchise" available at an attractive valuation. Despite a recent 25% year-to-date pullback, Blackstone's fundamentals remain strong with significant revenue and net income growth in 2025, and robust inflows. The upgrade, supported by a $154 price target, suggests that recent market "dislocation" does not reflect the company's underlying health and growth potential.

feed.title: Video: Huntington Bank: ‘3 Things We Learned this Week’
feed.summary: This article features a video from Huntington Bank discussing "The Three Things We Learned This Week" which is a regular blog post from Huntington officials. In this week's video, Randy Hare, Director of Equity Research at Huntington Bank, analyzes market and economy insights, covering volatility across three key markets. He also shares findings on new personal spending and income data, and discusses how market stability is being rewarded beyond just mega-cap stocks.

feed.title: YPF awards Halliburton multibillion-dollar long-term unconventional completions contract in Argentina
feed.summary: Halliburton has been awarded a multibillion-dollar, multiyear contract by YPF to provide unconventional completions services in Argentina's Vaca Muerta shale. This agreement marks the first international deployment of Halliburton's ZEUS® electric fracturing services and includes its OCTIV® Auto Frac service, combining electrification, automation, and advanced digital workflows to boost efficiency and reduce emissions. The collaboration aims to set a new standard for unconventional fracturing internationally.

feed.title: Stryker to buy Amplitude Vascular Systems
feed.summary: Stryker announced its agreement to acquire Amplitude Vascular Systems (AVS), a firm specializing in intravascular lithotripsy (IVL) for treating artery disease, though the financial terms were not disclosed. The acquisition aims to strengthen Stryker's peripheral vascular portfolio with AVS's Pulse IVL system, which is seen as a potential challenger to existing IVL technologies by competitors like Johnson & Johnson and Boston Scientific. This move highlights Stryker's increasing focus on cardiovascular treatments and high-growth markets through strategic acquisitions.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) Price Target Raised to $67.00 at UBS Group
feed.summary: UBS Group has increased its price target for Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) to $67.00 from $64.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating, which implies a potential 14.4% upside. Several other brokerage firms also raised their price targets for OXY, though MarketBeat's consensus rating remains a "Hold" with an average target of $60.91. The company recently reported Q4 earnings per share of $0.31, beating estimates, but revenue of $5.11 billion missed expectations.

feed.title: Conagra Brands shares slide as CEO Connolly ousted
feed.summary: Conagra Brands (NYSE:CAG) shares dropped over 5.6% after the company announced CEO Sean Connolly would step down, with John Brase taking over. The leadership change comes amid a significant stock price slide, weak sales, and industry challenges including inflation, shifting consumer habits, and the impact of weight-loss drugs. While analysts view the new appointment constructively, they caution that deeper structural issues like high debt and margin compression remain.

feed.title: Hilton Hotel: Between Travel Boom and Rising Costs, Profitability in Focus
feed.summary: Hilton Hotel faces shifting dynamics in a recovering global travel market, balancing strong occupancy gains with inflation pressures. The company is relying on its asset-light model, aggressive brand expansion in underserved markets, and its robust loyalty program to maintain profitability. Despite risks like rising interest rates and labor shortages, analysts view Hilton Worldwide favorably, citing strong EPS growth potential.

feed.title: Brown & Brown Inc. stock (US1156371007): Why its insurance brokerage scale now matters more for U.S.
feed.summary: Brown & Brown Inc. is an insurance brokerage powerhouse that acts as an intermediary connecting clients with insurance carriers, earning commissions without taking on underwriting risk. This asset-light model allows the company to scale through acquisitions and organic growth, generating predictable cash flows. Its focus on U.S. markets, disciplined M&A strategy, and adaptation to emerging risks like AI and trade volatility make it a resilient investment opportunity for U.S. and English-speaking investors.

feed.title: U.S. drinking water systems need up to $2.4T over 25 years
feed.summary: American Water (NYSE: AWK) highlighted a new AWWA report stating that U.S. drinking water systems require $2.1-$2.4 trillion in investment over 25 years to modernize and sustain operations. American Water pledged to invest up to $48 billion itself over the next decade to support its 14 million customers. The report also identified a $56.6 billion annual funding gap and warned that household water bills could double by 2050 if the issue is not addressed.

feed.title: Textron Aviation Defense Awarded Five-Year Sustaining Engineering and Program Management Contract for Beechcraft T-6 Texan II Fleet
feed.summary: Textron Aviation Defense LLC has secured a five-year U.S. government contract valued at over $150,000,000 for Sustaining Engineering and Program Management (SEPM) services for the U.S. Air Force, Navy, and Army T-6 Texan II aircraft fleet. This extends a previous contract, bringing the total cumulative value to $510,000,000, and ensures ongoing support for the mission readiness of the T-6 Texan II fleet, which is the world's leading military flight trainer.

feed.title: Ford recalls nearly 423,000 trucks, SUVs for wiper arm defect
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. has issued a recall for nearly 423,000 SUVs and pickup trucks due to potentially defective windshield wiper arms that may break, leading to compromised driver visibility. The recall includes certain 2021-2023 Lincoln Navigator and Ford Expedition SUVs, and various F-Series Super Duty trucks produced between October 2021 and December 2022. Dealers will replace the faulty wiper arms free of charge, with interim owner notifications starting April 13, 2026.

feed.title: Multimillion-dollar Settlement Against Walt Disney Company Approved
feed.summary: A federal judge has preliminarily approved a $50 million class action settlement against The Walt Disney Company. This settlement addresses claims from DirecTV and YouTube TV subscribers who alleged overpayment for services due to Disney's carriage agreements and ESPN bundling practices, which they argue violated the Sherman Antitrust Act. The final approval hearing is scheduled for 2027, with millions of subscribers potentially eligible to claim part of the settlement.

feed.title: Costco stock stabilizes after membership fee hike as volatility persists: weekly report
feed.summary: Costco's stock experienced a slight fall of 0.95% over the week, closing at $988.79, but maintained a strong bullish trend above its key moving averages. The company's recent membership fee hike and robust renewal rates are underpinning positive sentiment, despite mixed short-term technical signals suggesting potential consolidation. Analysts recommend watching the $1,000 resistance level for a confirmed bullish breakout in the coming week.

feed.title: Iris Energy Stock Jumps on Bold AI and Mining Bet
feed.summary: Iris Energy (IREN) shares are rising significantly due to unexpected growth in its Bitcoin mining operations and expansion into AI-focused data centers. The company increased its 2024 year-end hash rate target from 20 EH/s to 30 EH/s, reflecting greater capacity. This dual strategy of increased crypto-mining expectations and new AI growth avenues is attracting fresh capital and supporting the stock's rally.

feed.title: Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Group Boosts IDEXX Laboratories Stake
feed.summary: Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Group Inc. increased its holdings in IDEXX Laboratories, Inc. by 4.3% in the fourth quarter of 2022, bringing their total to 209,106 shares valued at $141,466,000. This move highlights IDEXX Laboratories' strong market position and growth potential in the animal health, water testing, and food safety diagnostic markets. The company's innovative solutions continue to attract major institutional investors.

feed.title: IDACORP Inc stock (US4511071064): Why utility stability matters more now for investors
feed.summary: IDACORP Inc. (US4511071064) is highlighted as a stable investment in a volatile market due to its regulated utility business, Idaho Power, which provides electricity to over 600,000 customers in Idaho and Oregon. The company benefits from population growth in its service territory, a strong focus on renewable energy, and a conservative financial strategy with consistent dividend growth. Its defensive position against economic downturns and potential for federal clean energy incentives make it an appealing long-term holding for investors seeking reliable income and modest appreciation.

feed.title: UBS Raises Price Target on Pfizer to $27 From $25, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: UBS has increased its price target for Pfizer (PFE) shares to $27, up from the previous $25, while reiterating a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a modest upside potential for the pharmaceutical giant. The analysis suggests that despite a higher price target, UBS maintains a cautious stance on Pfizer's near-term outlook.

feed.title: GSK (NYSE: GSK) leaders reinvest April 2026 dividend into company shares
feed.summary: Several senior executives and associated individuals at GSK plc acquired additional GSK ordinary shares through dividend reinvestment on April 9 and 10, 2026. The transactions involved dividends paid on April 9 being automatically used to purchase shares on the London Stock Exchange at prices ranging from £21.50 to £21.70. CEO Luke Miels, President Corporate Development David Redfern, and other key leaders were among those who reinvested their dividends, indicating their continued confidence in the company.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Double Downgrades Best Buy to Sell as Memory Cost Headwinds Threaten Margins
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs double downgraded Best Buy (BBY) stock from Buy to Sell, lowering its price target to $59 from $76 due to concerns about margin compression. The downgrade is primarily driven by anticipated higher memory costs impacting laptop and computer prices, which Goldman believes will lead to lower sales volumes and reduced margins for Best Buy, a significant portion of whose revenue comes from computing and mobile phones. This move contrasts with the generally more optimistic consensus among other analysts, though Best Buy's own management had previously flagged concerns about declining operating income rates.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Double Downgrades Best Buy to Sell as Memory Cost Headwinds Threaten Margins
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has double downgraded Best Buy (BBY) from Buy to Sell, lowering its price target to $59 due to concerns over rising memory costs impacting margins and sales. The analyst forecasts that higher memory component prices will force manufacturers to absorb costs or pass them to consumers, potentially leading to lower-priced model purchases and reduced unit volumes, severely affecting Best Buy's revenue and profitability, especially since computing and mobile phones constitute 47% of its domestic revenue. This downgrade, despite a more constructive broader analyst sentiment, highlights significant pressure on Best Buy's already declining operating income and struggles in key product categories.

feed.title: Royal Bank Of Canada Lowers Equity Residential Price Target
feed.summary: Royal Bank Of Canada has lowered its price target on Equity Residential (NYSE:EQR) from $68.00 to $67.00, while maintaining an 'outperform' rating. The new target represents a potential upside of 10.43% for the real estate investment trust. This adjustment reflects ongoing volatility in the REIT sector and analysts' evaluations of economic conditions on rental demand.

feed.title: BlackRock Continues Bitcoin Buying Spree with $612 Million Added Through IBIT ETF
feed.summary: BlackRock's IBIT Bitcoin ETF significantly increased its Bitcoin holdings by an additional $612 million, demonstrating sustained institutional interest in the cryptocurrency. This buying spree occurred despite BlackRock IBIT holders sitting on estimated unrealized losses of $12 billion, with an average cost basis of $89,000 per Bitcoin. The ongoing inflows suggest a potential stabilization and floor for Bitcoin prices, solidifying BlackRock's role as a major institutional buyer.
